verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: N envelopes coexist; No cross-session leakage; Shared durable plane safe; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red against the pre-delivery surface (engine absent), green at HEAD (1/1)
gate_result.coexist_evidence: Three envelopes (alpha/beta/gamma) run concurrently on the shared kernel, overlapping in time (each sleeps mid-run), all exiting 0.
gate_result.leakage_evidence: While all three ran, each envelope's /tmp listing contained ONLY its own private file — none of the other envelopes' state was visible in any session (private mount namespaces per envelope).
gate_result.durable_evidence: All three appended to the same durable bind concurrently; the shared log contains exactly one line from each (atomic O_APPEND single-line writes), and every envelope tore down leakcheck-clean.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Three real concurrent bwrap envelopes with overlapping lifetimes on this host.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-070 is complete: multiple envelopes coexist on one shared kernel without state collision — three concurrent envelopes each saw only their own private /tmp, the shared durable plane received exactly one record from each without loss or interleaving corruption, and all tore down with zero leaks.
